meta.title: Component System - Frontend Architecture
meta.excerpt: A structured overview of atoms, molecules, organisms, and templates in the frontend architecture.

seo.metaDescription: Understand the frontend component hierarchy and design patterns.

blocks.content: The component system uses atomic design to maximize reuse and consistency across pages.

features.title: Atoms
features.description: Smallest UI primitives like buttons, labels, and inputs.

features.title: Molecules
features.description: Composed groups of atoms solving focused UI tasks.

features.title: Organisms
features.description: Complex, reusable interface sections built from molecules.

blocks.title: Atomic Design Methodology
blocks.content: This system follows Brad Frost's atomic design methodology, creating a hierarchical component architecture from simple to complex.
Atoms are the foundational building blocks of the UI system. They are the smallest, most basic components that can't be broken down further without losing their meaning.

features.title: Button
features.description: Primary, secondary, ghost, and destructive variants

features.title: Typography
features.description: Heading, paragraph, label, and code text components

features.title: Input
features.description: Text, email, password, number, and textarea fields

features.title: Icon
features.description: SVG icon system with consistent sizing

features.title: Badge
features.description: Status indicators and tags

features.title: Checkbox
features.description: Boolean selection with label support

blocks.title: Button Atom Example

blocks.content: Molecules combine multiple atoms to create functional UI patterns. They solve specific UI problems by grouping atoms with a defined purpose.

features.title: FormField
features.description: Label + Input + Error message composition

features.title: SearchBar
features.description: Input field with search icon and clear button

features.title: Card
features.description: Container with header, content, and footer sections

features.title: Dropdown
features.description: Button trigger with menu items list

features.title: Alert
features.description: Icon + Title + Description for notifications

features.title: Breadcrumb
features.description: Navigation trail with separator icons

blocks.title: FormField Molecule Example
Molecule Composition
blocks.content: Molecules should be self-contained and reusable across different contexts. They shouldn't depend on specific page layouts.
Organisms are complex UI sections built from molecules and atoms. They represent distinct sections of an interface with specific functionality.

features.title: Header
features.description: Logo, navigation menu, search, and user actions

features.title: Footer
features.description: Site map, social links, legal, and newsletter signup

features.title: ArticleList
features.description: Grid of article cards with pagination

features.title: ContactForm
features.description: Complete form with validation and submission

features.title: Dashboard
features.description: Stats cards, charts, and data tables

features.title: CommentSection
features.description: Comment list with reply threads and form

blocks.title: Header Organism Example

blocks.content: Templates define page-level layouts by composing organisms and molecules. They establish the overall structure and grid system for pages.

blocks.title: Marketing Layout
blocks.content: Header + Hero + Features + Testimonials + CTA + Footer

blocks.title: Dashboard Layout
blocks.content: Sidebar navigation + Top bar + Main content area + Footer

blocks.title: Documentation Layout
blocks.content: Header + Sidebar navigation + Content + Table of contents + Footer

blocks.title: Documentation Template Example
Template Consistency
blocks.content: Templates ensure consistent layouts across the application while allowing flexibility in content composition.
Follow these best practices when creating and using components in the system.

items: Keep components focused on a single responsibility
Use TypeScript interfaces for props with clear documentation
Implement proper error boundaries at organism level
Follow the atomic hierarchy when creating new components
Use composition over inheritance for component reuse
Implement proper accessibility (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation)
Keep styles scoped using Tailwind classes or CSS modules
Extract shared logic into custom hooks
Document component APIs with JSDoc comments
Write unit tests for complex component logic

blocks.title: Anti-Pattern: Skip Levels
blocks.content: Avoid having atoms directly import organisms or templates. Always follow the hierarchy: atoms → molecules → organisms → templates.

features.title: Type Safety
features.description: Use TypeScript for all component props and state

features.title: Testing
features.description: Write unit tests for logic, integration tests for pages

features.title: Accessibility
features.description: Ensure keyboard navigation and screen reader support

features.title: Performance
features.description: Use React.memo and lazy loading where appropriate
